zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan Radcliffe於1429年出生時，她的父親Thomas Radcliffe38歲，母親Catherine Booth39歲。她於1445年與Sir Robert Sherburne在英國藍卡爾結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1465年在英國藍卡爾斯托爾斯特享年36歲。

nameMeanings.name: Radcliffe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Ratliff
meanings: English (Suffolk): habitational name from one or more of the places so named, such as Radcliffe (Lancashire), Radcliffe on Trent and Ratcliffe on Soar (both Nottinghamshire), Radclive (Buckinghamshire), Ratcliffe Culey and Ratcliffe on the Wreake (both Leicestershire), Ratclyffe in Clyst Hydon, Ratcliffes in Thorverton, and Ratcliffes in Broad Clyst (all Devon), Ratcliff in Stepney (Middlesex), and Rackley in Compton Bishop (Somerset). The placenames derive from Old English rēad ‘red’ + clif ‘cliff, bank, steep slope’ (see also Rutley ). A family of the name Radcliffe trace their descent from Sir Nicholas de Radclyffe. He is said to have been a knight who held the major of Radcliffe in Lancashire and served Roger de Poitou, Baron de Marsey in the 11th century.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan Radcliffe was born in 1429, her father, Thomas Radcliffe, was 38 and her mother, Catherine Booth, was 39. She married Sir Robert Sherburne in 1445, in Lancashire, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. She died in 1465, in Stonyhurst, Lancashire, England, at the age of 36.

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Radcliffe(이)가 1429년에 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas Radcliffe(은)는 38세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Catherine Booth(은)는 39세였다. 그녀는 1445년 영국 랭카실르에서 Sir Robert Sherburne(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1465년 영국 랭카실르 스토니후르스트에서 36세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan Radcliffe родилась в 1429 г., её отцу по имени Thomas Radcliffe было 38 лет, а матери, которую звали Catherine Booth, 39. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Robert Sherburne в 1445 г. в местности ланкашир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1465 г. в местности стонихурст, ланкашир, Англия в возрасте 36 лет.
